title: Max Smart Super Speciality Hospital Saket launches 400-bed tower in Delhi-NCR
summary: New facility to expand capacity and support medical value travel under Heal in India initiative
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
New facility to expand capacity and support medical value travel under Heal in India initiative

Max Smart Super Speciality Hospital Saket announced the launch of a newly built 400-bed tower aimed at delivering healthcare services in Delhi-NCR.

The facility integrates medical technology, digital systems, and care standards to support patient care for domestic and international patients.

The new tower is expected to increase the hospital’s capacity to meet healthcare demand in Delhi-NCR and support India’s medical value travel sector under the ‘Heal in India’ initiative. The hospital is part of the Max Healthcare Institute network.

Max Healthcare has outlined a plan to build Centres of Excellence and expand its bed capacity to 10,000 over the next four years to address patient volumes and occupancy levels across its network.

Abhay Soi, Chairman and Managing Director, Max Healthcare Institute, said; “This state-of-the-art new facility brings together cutting-edge technology, world-class infrastructure, and the finest Indian clinical talent to deliver exceptional patient care for people across the globe. I am confident that this new facility will help establish Delhi-NCR as the hub for medical value travel. This is in line with the Government’s vision to establish India as the leading destination for medical tourism, supported by the ‘Heal in India’ initiative.”

With the addition of the new tower, the Max Hospital, Saket complex will have a total capacity of over 1,160 beds across more than 35 specialties.

The tower includes over 120 critical care beds, 10 modular operating theatres, and Cath Labs, supporting care across Cardiac Sciences, Oncology, Renal Sciences, Neurosciences, Gastroenterology, Paediatrics, Robotic Surgery, and Organ Transplants.

The hospital is equipped with technologies including the Da Vinci Xi Surgical Robot and the Mako Total Knee Replacement Robot, supporting procedures and patient outcomes.
